description: The implementation-start gate (scripts/implementation_authorization.py validate_project_authorization_row) MUST authorize a cited work item under restrictive semantics: if excluded then blocked; else if included_work_item_ids is non-empty then authorized iff the work item is listed (active project membership is neither required nor sufficient); else (empty list) then authorized iff active project member. This tightens the prior listed-OR-member logic.

The bridge Write-time gate (.claude/hooks/bridge-compliance-gate.py _wi_project_membership_gap) MUST apply identical restrictive semantics to A1: a non-empty included_work_item_ids list is authoritative, so a listed work item is authorized even without a recorded active membership row; an empty list falls back to active project membership.
excluded_work_item_ids takes precedence at BOTH gates: a work item present in excluded_work_item_ids is blocked regardless of inclusion or project membership.

A spec-derived test asserts the implementation-start gate and the bridge Write-time gate produce the same authorize-or-block verdict across the (member, listed) truth table for non-empty and empty included lists.
